title: Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus

herb_names_common.name: new mexico milkvetch (astragalus kentrophyta ssp. neomexicanus)

what_description: Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial herbaceous plant with taproot, growing up to 60 cm in height and 30 cm in width, with hairy stems and leaves. The leaves are lance-shaped, 2-5 cm long, and 0.5-2 cm wide, with 3-9 pairs of leaflets. The inflorescence is a spike with 2-8 flowers, each 1-1.5 cm long, pink or purple in color, with 5 petals and 10 stamens. The fruit is a flat, hairy pod, 2-3 cm long, containing 1-2 seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus are inflammation, chronic fatigue, and diabetes, due to its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to increase insulin sensitivity. It has also been shown to lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension by reducing vascular resistance, and has been found to inhibit cancer cell growth, potentially preventing cancer. Furthermore, Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus has been effective in boosting the immune system, alleviating digestive issues by regulating gut bacteria, and reducing the symptoms of arthritis by suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, it has been used to treat anemia by increasing red blood cell production, respiratory issues by increasing lung function, and bronchitis by reducing inflammation and mucus production. It also possesses antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating skin conditions, skin infections, kidney disease, and cardiovascular disease, while also serving as an adaptogen to reduce stress and anxiety, and a natural expectorant to alleviate cough and fever.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus are primarily associated with its antioxidant activity, which is mainly attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in the plant. This antioxidant property is further enhanced by the plant's immunomodulatory effect, which is mediated by saponins and other bioactive compounds that modulate the immune response. The anti-inflammatory activity of Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus is responsible for its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis, and is attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. In addition to its anti-inflammatory properties, the plant exhibits antimicrobial activity, which is largely due to the presence of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that inhibit the growth of microorganisms. Moreover, Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus has been found to possess cardioprotective properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular diseases.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus are a group of triterpene saponins, specifically astragaloside IV, astragaloside II, astragaloside VI, astragaloside III, and astragaloside VIII, which have been identified as the primary bioactive compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. Astragaloside IV is known for its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, while astragaloside II exhibits antioxidant and anti-apoptotic activities. Astragaloside VI has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties, and astragaloside III has been found to have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant effects. Astragaloside VIII, on the other hand, has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties. These compounds have been isolated and identified through various studies and their mechanisms of action have been elucidated, highlighting their potential therapeutic applications.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus are tea, astragalus tincture, infusion, capsules, and decoction. Tea is prepared by steeping dried roots in hot water to extract its bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. Astragalus tincture is a concentrated liquid extract of the plant's roots, typically made with ethanol or glycerin, which can be used to support the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. Capsules are filled with dried and powdered root material, providing a convenient and standardized way to consume the plant's active compounds. Decoction is a method of extracting the plant's bioactive compounds by boiling the dried roots in water for an extended period, which can be used to prepare a strong tea or as a base for other herbal preparations.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus are experiencing allergic reactions, which can be severe and potentially life-threatening, due to the plant's allergenic properties and the release of histamine, a chemical that causes allergic reactions. Additionally, Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus may interact with medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, leading to adverse effects, including bleeding complications and hypoglycemia. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can cause gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, due to the saponins and other compounds that can irritate the digestive system. Some individuals may also develop allergic reactions, such as hives and itching, after exposure to the plant's allergenic compounds. Furthermore, Astragalus kentrophyta subsp. neomexicanus may cause skin irritation, including redness and itching, particularly in individuals with pre-existing skin conditions or allergies.
